title: Microvascular Permeability and Lipoprotein Flux Protocol
author: John Rutledge
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.56vg9e6

steps.title: Mice are anesthetized with an intraperitoneal...
steps.instruction: Mice are anesthetized with an intraperitoneal injection with 50 mg pentobarbital/kg weight.

steps.title: All treatments are administered into the...
steps.instruction: All treatments are administered into the left femoral vein by bolus injection. FITC-albumin (40 mg/mL) in 100 µL: a. PBS b. VLDL (150 mg/dL) c. VLDL (150mg/dL) + LpL (2 U/mL) d. LpL (2 U/mL) in PBS

steps.title: Alternatively, the mouse is then infused...
steps.instruction: Alternatively, the mouse is then infused at with 100 uL fluorescently labeled compound alone (FLC, see above) (40 mg/mL)

steps.title: Excess FLC was removed from the...
steps.instruction: Excess FLC was removed from the vasculature by infusion with DMEM media for 20 min by infusion into the left ventricle of heart and followed by infusion of 10% formaldehyde for 20 min.

steps.title: The microvascular rich tissues interest are...
steps.instruction: The microvascular rich tissues interest are immediately removed and fixed in 10% formaldehyde for two days. a. microvascular tissues = brain, heart, and mesentery ect. b. macrovascular tissues= common carotid arteries or aorta

steps.title: The tissue is embedded in paraffin...
steps.instruction: The tissue is embedded in paraffin and sectioned to 5 µm thickness.

steps.title: Tissues sections are deparaffinized, rehydrated, and imaged using fluorescent microscopy
steps.instruction: Tissues sections are deparaffinized, rehydrated, and imaged using fluorescent microscopy.
